steps.title: Identify the target meaning
steps.content: The passage states writers "have drawn on these stories to craft a rich portrait." The word must convey that traditional stories are the source for modern Hawaiian literature.

steps.title: Match the word to the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs a phrase meaning "originated from" or "used as a source" — modern Hawaiian literature has ______ the traditional stories of the Kānaka Maoli. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — brooded on: modern Hawaiian literature as having brooded on traditional stories — brooded on means to dwell on something anxiously, suggesting rumination rather than creative derivation. ✗
  • Choice B — supplanted: modern Hawaiian literature as having supplanted traditional stories — supplanted means replaced, but the passage says writers drew on these stories, not pushed them aside. ✗
  • Choice C — anticipated: modern Hawaiian literature as having anticipated traditional stories — anticipated means predicted or preceded, which reverses the timeline (the traditional stories came first). ✗
  • Choice D — derived from: modern Hawaiian literature as having derived from traditional stories — derived from means originated or developed from a source, matching that writers "drawn on these stories" to create their works. ✓
D is correct.
